`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go`, `PluginDispatch`: this resolver calls `dispatchPluginAction` synchronously at line 122 and creates a `.ddx/plugin-dispatches` record id at line 126. It does not start a `WorkerManager` worker or emit `workerProgress`, so `+page.svelte:225` subscribes to an id that will never produce terminal events.
`cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/plugins/+page.svelte`, failure handling: the per-card failure badge at lines 391-403 depends on receiving a `failed`/`error` worker phase, but plugin install/update failures currently return as synchronous GraphQL errors from `resolver_feat008.go:123`, leaving only the global `dispatchError` path.

post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)

`cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/plugins/+page.svelte`, failure handling: the per-card failure badge at lines 391-403 depends on receiving a `failed`/`error` worker phase, but plugin install/update failures currently return as synchronous GraphQL errors from `resolver_feat008.go:123`, leaving only the global `dispatchError` path.

post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES
`cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/plugins/+page.svelte`, `handleWorkerTerminal`: on failure phases, the function calls `refreshPlugins()` before `clearInFlight()` and `workerFailures.set(...)`. Preserve the stored `dispatchSnapshots` state for that plugin on failure, or skip/apply refresh only for success phases, so a failed worker cannot make the card appear successfully updated.

**No source changes at all.** The diff adds one JSON file recording execution metadata. The plugins page (`cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/plugins/+page.svelte`) is untouched.
**The `result.json` reports `"outcome": "task_succeeded"`**, but the merged commit (`92e12099`) that it references (`fix(plugins): skip plugin refresh on worker failure phases`) appears to be a narrow fix unrelated to the full AC scope — and even that commit's changes are not included in this diff.
**All 9 ACs require frontend implementation** (Map-based tracking, subscribeWorkerProgress integration, polling fallback, spinner UI, error badges, Playwright tests). None of this work is present.

pre-execute-bead checkpoint: staging changes: exit status 128

No changes required.

The bead's acceptance criteria are already fully satisfied by code present at
base revision 83b98c1c3e1c43e2e131635eef19f0861760439c (the execute-bead
starting point for this attempt). The bead was re-queued with stale REVIEW:BLOCK
findings from a prior attempt, but those findings have already been addressed
by commits that are part of base:

  - 7133371f fix: run plugin dispatches as workers [ddx-7de0ce80]
  - efd5bacb fix: refresh plugin cards after dispatch [ddx-7de0ce80]
  - 92e12099 fix(plugins): skip plugin refresh on worker failure phases [ddx-7de0ce80]

Mapping from AC items to implementation at base:

AC1 (per-card in-flight tracking):
  cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/plugins/+page.svelte
  lines 56-60: dispatchingPlugins Set, inFlightWorkers Map<name,workerId>,
  inFlightActions, dispatchSnapshots, workerFailures.
  lines 115-117: isBusy(name) gate.
  lines 458-477: Install/Update buttons disabled while busy.

AC2 (worker-driven refresh):
  +page.svelte lines 225-238 (subscribeToWorker), 240-258 (handleWorkerTerminal).
  On non-failure terminal phase, refreshPlugins() runs, then clearInFlight(name).

AC3 (fallback polling):
  +page.svelte lines 190-223. scheduleFallbackPolling sets a 30s timer
  (configurable via __ddxPluginFallbackDelayMs). When it fires without a
  terminal event, startFallbackPolling polls every 2s until the snapshot
  changes. Playwright test at e2e/plugins.spec.ts:349 ("plugin update falls
  back to polling when worker stream drops terminal event") exercises this.

AC4 (in-flight visual state):
  +page.svelte lines 377-383. Loader2 spinner + "Installing..."/"Updating..."
  label replaces the status badge while workerId && action are set. Button
  disabled with Tooltip pointing to worker.

AC5 (failure surfacing):
  +page.svelte lines 392-405. workerFailures Map stores {workerId, action}
  on failure phases; renders red AlertCircle badge with link to worker page
  and title "Update failed"/"Install failed". handleWorkerTerminal (line 247)
  returns early on failure phases without calling refreshPlugins, preserving
  pre-dispatch state. dispatchError remains for synchronous mutation errors
  only (lines 287-289, 338-343).

AC6 (concurrent dispatch):
  Map-keyed tracking allows independent state per plugin. Header at
  +page.svelte lines 318-335 iterates inFlightWorkers.entries() and renders
  one link per active worker. Playwright test at e2e/plugins.spec.ts:381
  ("two plugin updates track separate workers concurrently").

AC7 (no extra refetch overhead):
  refreshPlugins (+page.svelte:184-188) calls only PLUGINS_LIST_QUERY, not
  the full page data loader.

AC8 (Playwright e2e):
  cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/plugins.spec.ts has dedicated tests
  covering in-flight state (line 284 "plugin update refreshes card when
  worker completes successfully"), failure (line 314), polling fallback
  (line 349), concurrent (line 381). Uses page.routeWebSocket to simulate
  the workerProgress subscription.

AC9 (no regressions):
  Install flow through ConfirmDialog uses the same dispatchPlugin() code
  path (lines 295-301), so install also gets in-flight tracking.
  Existing install test at e2e/plugins.spec.ts:199 (US-098.b) still asserts
  the Install flow.

Backend wiring:
  cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:119-145 — adapter uses
  WorkerManager.StartPluginAction with an executor callback.
  cli/internal/server/workers.go:393-519 (StartPluginAction + runPluginAction)
  — emits a "running" event at start and a "done" or "failed" phase event
  when DispatchPluginAction returns. Verified by
  TestWorkerManagerStartPluginActionPublishesTerminalProgress and
  TestWorkerManagerStartPluginActionPublishesFailureProgress
  (cli/internal/server/workers_test.go:47,93). Both pass locally.

Verification run in this worktree:
  - `go build ./...` in cli/: clean.
  - `go test ./internal/server/ -run 'Plugin' -v`: both worker tests PASS.

Because no code change is needed, committing a no-op would violate the
execute-bead instruction "Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code."
This rationale records the analysis so a reviewer can confirm and close the
bead, or reopen with a more specific gap to address.
